PRUITTHEALTH - BROOKHAVEN

Overview

Address
3535 ASHTON WOODS DRIVE NE, ATLANTA, GA 30319
Phone
7704510236
Certified beds
157
Avg daily residents
116 (74% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1984-02-01
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.53 4.78 2.84 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.76 0.84 0.61 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.65 1.06 0.52 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.12 2.89 1.71 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.40 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.13 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.05
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.55
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.46
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
45.7%
annual
RN turnover
38.9%
annual
Administrators departed
New facility — insufficient data.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.69
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.23
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-11-07 14 14 14 56 1 56
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-06-16 28 3 25 491 1 491
Total weighted health score
164.8
lower is better
Infection-control citations
19
last cycle

Deficiencies (48)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

42 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
6 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0568 Properly hold, secure, and manage each resident's personal money which is deposited with the nursing home. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. D 2024-11-07 2024-12-09
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. E 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0826 Provide specialized rehabilitative services by qualified personnel, when ordered for a resident by a doctor. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. E 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0940 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program for all new and existing staff members. D 2024-06-04 2024-07-19
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. J 2023-08-06 2023-08-30
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. J 2023-08-06 2023-08-30
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. J 2023-08-06 2023-08-30
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2023-08-06 2023-08-30
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. J 2023-08-06 2023-08-30
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. J 2023-08-06 2023-08-30
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2022-06-16 2022-08-09
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2022-06-16 2022-08-09
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. E 2022-06-16 2022-08-09
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2019-11-21 2020-01-05
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2019-11-21 2020-01-05
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2019-11-21 2020-01-05
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2019-11-21 2020-01-05
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2019-11-21 2020-01-05
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2019-11-21 2020-01-05
